target.title: chore(deps): refresh development toolchain
target.body:
## Summary

- Update `@types/node` from 25.9.2 to 25.9.3.
- Advance the already-pinned native TypeScript preview from the June 9 build to the June 11 build.
- Keep pnpm on major 10 and update from 10.33.2 to 10.34.3.
- Regenerate the lockfile and record the maintenance update in the unreleased changelog.

## Dependency audit

Registry and upstream metadata checked on June 12, 2026.

| Package/tool | Audited state | Decision |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `@fastify/websocket` | 11.2.0 is current | Keep |
| `commander` | 14.0.3 installed; 15.0.0 available | Defer major |
| `dotenv` | 17.4.2 is current | Keep |
| `fastify` | 5.8.5 is current | Keep |
| `ws` | 8.21.0 is current | Keep |
| `@types/node` | 25.9.2 to 25.9.3 | Update |
| `@types/ws` | 8.18.1 is current | Keep |
| `@typescript/native-preview` | June 9 build to June 11 build | Update existing preview pin |
| `oxfmt` | 0.54.0 is current | Keep |
| `oxlint` | 1.69.0 is current | Keep |
| `tsup` | 8.5.1 is current | Keep |
| `tsx` | 4.22.4 is current | Keep |
| `typescript` | 6.0.3 is current stable | Keep |
| `pnpm` | 10.33.2 to latest 10.x, 10.34.3; 11.6.0 available | Update within existing major |
| `actions/checkout` | v6 points to 6.0.3 | Keep |
| `actions/setup-node` | v6 points to 6.4.0 | Keep |

Commander 15 is intentionally excluded. Its [official release notes](https://github.com/tj/commander.js/releases/tag/v15.0.0) document an ESM-only implementation, changed negative-option default behavior, and a Node.js 22.12.0 minimum. Vox currently supports generic Node.js 22 and Commander 14 remains security-supported through May 2027, so taking the major would widen this maintenance patch into a runtime-contract change.

pnpm 10.34.3 includes a [security hardening release](https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/releases/tag/v10.34.3) for repository-controlled npm configuration. Vox has no project `.npmrc` or workspace config requiring migration. pnpm 11 is deferred as a package-manager major.

The native preview delta is 12 upstream commits. The [official comparison](https://github.com/microsoft/typescript-go/compare/69b0d53976ef291fa0bb2b636ec1b557a015f6b3...cda7baffa96f0534d10f4cf4b909c0d06542cc0d) is primarily compiler correctness, declaration emit, Unicode handling, and config diagnostics.

All audited package repositories are active and none of the selected packages are deprecated. `pnpm audit` reports zero vulnerabilities across 242 resolved dependencies.

## Verification

- Frozen install under pnpm 10.34.3.
- Formatting and lint clean.
- Native TypeScript typecheck clean.
- 33/33 tests pass.
- Build succeeds.
- Focused logger/server teardown and playback tests: 10/10 pass.
- Built `dist/cli.js`: root, `serve`, `dial`, and `simulate` help pass; invalid port, missing required option, and missing required argument fail correctly.
- Built server starts, serves `{"ok":true}` from `/health`, and terminates cleanly.
- Package tarball SHA-256: `88dc5902fe80ebdde63773938dc6726187f6cbac15486e1bdc38253bd1632387`.
- Structured autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

Real Twilio/OpenAI proof is not required for this candidate: no runtime dependency or integration code changes. The closest runtime boundary is the exact built CLI/server lifecycle above, supplemented by production logger/subprocess teardown tests.

## Public Model Identifier Gate

PASS across the exact diff, tests, workflow, full-gate logs, built output, package tarball, and this PR text. Existing artifact identifiers were verified against the official OpenAI [Realtime model](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-realtime) and [transcription model](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-4o-transcribe) documentation.

## Risk

The native TypeScript checker remains a preview dependency. Risk is bounded by its exact version pin, clean typecheck, full test/build coverage, and the required Node.js 22/24 CI matrix. No production package changed.
